title:
Module 1
The Basics
subtitle: Course Overview and the Complete Basics to Starting AI Models

sections.header: What Is the AI Influencer Method

blocks.content: The AI Influencer Method is a system for building and monetizing social media pages using AI-generated personas. You create a fictional character — face, name, personality — using AI image and video tools, then grow that character's social media presence and convert followers into paying subscribers.

The persona looks and behaves like a real influencer. The audience follows for entertainment, attention, and content. You are running a media brand, not pretending to be a real person. The business works because the demand for parasocial content is massive and growing, and AI tools now produce output that is visually indistinguishable from real photos and videos.

left.content: You generate a base AI model (a consistent face), then produce images and videos of that character in different settings, outfits, and scenarios. You post this content across social media platforms to build an audience, then funnel that audience toward a paid subscription where they access exclusive content.

Every part of the system is controlled by you: the face, the brand, the content schedule, and the revenue. There is no creator dependency, no contracts, and no negotiations. You own the entire operation.

items: AI image tools (Seedream 4.5, Nano Banana 2) produce photorealistic output that passes as real
AI video tools (Kling) generate natural-looking video clips from single images
Social platforms reward high-volume, consistent content — AI delivers that at scale
One operator can run the entire pipeline: generation, editing, posting, monetization
Startup costs are under $500 — no studio, no equipment, no talent

blocks.content: Revenue does not come from posting content. It comes from moving people through a funnel. Each platform in this system has one job: push the audience one step closer to paying. No single platform generates money on its own — the system works as a connected chain.

items: Stage 1: Discovery — Free content on Instagram, TikTok, Threads, Reddit draws attention
Stage 2: Follow — The viewer likes the content enough to follow the account
Stage 3: Click — The follower taps the link in your bio to see what else exists
Stage 4: Warm — The follower joins your free Telegram channel, sees teaser content, builds desire
Stage 5: Convert — The warmed follower subscribes to your paid platform for exclusive access

left.content: Every piece of content you create exists to move someone from one stage to the next. A Reel on Instagram is not the product — it is the advertisement. The subscription is the product.

items: Instagram — Primary discovery and trust platform. Largest audience, highest conversion potential
Threads — Engagement engine that feeds Instagram's algorithm with activity
TikTok — Secondary discovery. Viral reach supplements Instagram growth
Reddit — Niche exposure inside specific communities. High intent traffic
Twitter/X — Brand presence and discovery through conversations and replies
Link in Bio — Bridge between free social media and paid conversion
Telegram — Warming layer. Followers see teaser content that builds desire before subscribing
Subscription Platform — Monetization endpoint where revenue is captured

blocks.content: Not every platform matters equally. Instagram is the foundation — everything else supports it. Your priority order determines where your time goes. Spreading effort equally across six platforms is a mistake. Concentrate on what drives revenue.

left.content: These three platforms form the core of your system. You cannot skip any of them. Instagram is the traffic engine, Threads amplifies Instagram's reach, and Telegram warms followers before conversion.

items: Instagram — Your most important platform. Reels, Stories, and carousels drive discovery. Profile visits convert to followers. This is where the majority of your funnel traffic originates
Threads — Text-based engagement platform linked directly to your Instagram. Threads activity increases your Instagram visibility. Post 3-4 times daily to generate profile visits
Telegram — Free channel where followers access teaser content. Acts as a warming layer between following you and paying. Post 2-3 times daily with content that creates desire for your paid platform

right.content: These platforms supplement your primary stack. They are not mandatory at launch. Add them once your Instagram and Threads system is stable and producing consistent growth.

items: TikTok — Short-form video platform with massive organic reach. Supplements Instagram by reaching a different audience segment. Carousel posts perform especially well for AI content

Reddit — Community-driven platform where niche content goes viral inside specific subreddits. High-intent traffic but requires careful warmup to avoid bans. Do not post links until karma is established
Twitter/X — Conversation-driven platform. Growth comes from replying to large accounts, not from posting original content. Requires Twitter Premium for meaningful visibility

right.title: Priority Rule
right.content: If you only have 2 hours per day, spend 100% of it on Instagram and Threads. Only expand to TikTok, Reddit, or Twitter/X when your primary platforms are running smoothly and you have bandwidth to add complexity.

blocks.content: The AI influencer model has a low barrier to entry. Total startup cost is approximately $200-$500. Most tools have free tiers that work for your first persona. Do not invest in premium tools until your first page is generating revenue.

items: Dedicated phone or device — $150-$400 (used phones work). One persona per device for account safety
Prepaid SIM card — $10-$30. Needed for phone verification on platform signups
VPN service — $5-$12 per month. Required for geo-targeting your content to US audiences
AI image generation credits (Seedream 4.5, Nano Banana 2) — $0-$50 per month depending on usage
Kling video credits — $10-$30 per month. Standard mode is sufficient for starting
Photopea — Free browser-based Photoshop alternative for image editing and artifact removal
CapCut — Free video editor. Handles cuts, transitions, trending audio, and text overlays
AllMyLinks or Linktree — Free link in bio pages for your conversion funnel
Google Sheets — Free content calendar and metrics tracking
Telegram — Free messaging platform for your warming channel

right.content: Every tool in this stack serves a specific function in the content pipeline. Modules 2-4 cover each tool in detail with step-by-step workflows.

items: Seedream 4.5 — Primary AI image generator. Produces photorealistic images from text prompts. Best for generating your base model and standard content
Nano Banana 2 — Secondary AI image generator. Stronger face consistency when using reference images. Use alongside Seedream for variety
Kling — AI video generator. Converts a single image into a realistic 5-10 second video clip. Standard and Professional modes available
Photopea — Image editor for fixing AI artifacts, adjusting lighting, and adding the 2% grain that makes images look real
CapCut — Video editor for trimming clips, adding trending audio, stitching multiple clips, and optimizing export settings per platform

blocks.content: Revenue builds slowly. The first 30 days are almost entirely setup and warmup with zero to minimal income. This is normal and expected. The operators earning $5K-$10K+ per month have been running their systems for 6-12 months.

items: Month 1: $0-$200 — Model creation, account setup, warmup protocols. Most of this month is preparation. Revenue is near zero
Month 2-3: $100-$500 — Accounts are live, content is flowing, first followers start converting. Revenue trickles in as the funnel begins working
Month 3-6: $500-$3,000 — Consistent posting and growing audience. Funnel is optimized. Revenue grows as follower count and trust increase
Month 6+: $2,000-$10,000+ — Scaled operations, possibly multiple personas. Revenue compounds as systems become efficient and audience compounds

left.title: Revenue Reality
left.content: Most operators earn under $500 in their first three months. This is normal. Do not compare your Month 1 to someone else's Month 12. The system works, but it requires patience and consistency.

right.content: Revenue scales based on three factors. Understanding which lever to pull at each stage prevents wasted effort.

items: Follower volume — More followers means more people entering your funnel. Driven by content quality and posting consistency
Conversion rate — Percentage of followers who become paying subscribers. Driven by funnel optimization, Telegram warming, and subscription page quality
Retention — How long subscribers stay. Driven by content quality on the paid platform and how regularly you post exclusive content

right.content: In Month 1-3, focus entirely on follower volume. You need audience before you can optimize conversion. In Month 3-6, shift focus to conversion rate and funnel optimization. In Month 6+, focus on retention and scaling to additional personas.

blocks.content: This is not passive income. During the first 3 months, expect to invest 2-4 hours per day, seven days per week. After Month 3-6, daily time drops to 1-2 hours once systems are built and content batching is established.

items: Content generation — 30-60 minutes. Batch image and video generation sessions. Aim to produce 3-5 days of content in one session
Posting and engagement — 30-60 minutes. Post across platforms, respond to comments, engage with other accounts to build algorithmic trust
Account management — 15-30 minutes. Check metrics, verify account health, monitor for shadowbans or warnings
Strategy and planning — 15-30 minutes. Plan content calendar, review what performed well, adjust posting schedule

left.title: Total Daily Investment
left.content: 2-4 hours per day during the first 3 months. After Month 3-6, this drops to 1-2 hours as content batching and systems become efficient. The early time investment builds the system that eventually runs lean.

right.content: The balance of tasks changes as your system matures. Early months are heavy on content creation and warmup. Later months shift toward engagement and optimization.

items: Month 1 — 70% content creation, 20% warmup and engagement, 10% setup and planning
Month 2-3 — 50% content creation, 30% posting and engagement, 20% funnel and strategy
Month 3-6 — 30% content creation (batched), 40% engagement and community, 30% optimization and scaling
Month 6+ — 20% content creation (highly batched), 30% engagement, 30% monetization optimization, 20% scaling to new personas

summary_points: The AI Influencer Method creates and monetizes fictional social media personas using AI image and video tools
Revenue comes from the funnel: Discovery → Follow → Click → Warm → Convert. Every platform serves a specific role
Instagram and Threads are mandatory primary platforms. TikTok, Reddit, and Twitter/X are support platforms added later
Startup cost is $200-$500. Core tools: Seedream 4.5, Nano Banana 2, Kling, Photopea, CapCut
Realistic revenue: $0-200 Month 1, $500-3K by Month 3-6, $2K-10K+ at Month 6+ with scaled operations
Daily time investment: 2-4 hours for first 3 months, dropping to 1-2 hours once systems are established
